theorem_title: Pinsker's inequality via reduction to the Bernoulli case
theorem_statement:
Pinsker's inequality via reduction to the Bernoulli case.

Let \((X,\mathcal F)\) be a measurable space, and let \(P\) and \(Q\) be probability measures on \((X,\mathcal F)\). Assume \(P\ll Q\), so that the Kullback–Leibler divergence
\[
D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\|Q)
:=
\int_X \log\!\left(\frac{dP}{dQ}\right)\,dP
\]
is well defined in \([0,\infty]\).

Then
\[
D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\|Q)\ge 2\,\|P-Q\|_{\mathrm{TV}}^2,
\]
where
\[
\|P-Q\|_{\mathrm{TV}}:=\sup_{A\in\mathcal F}|P(A)-Q(A)|.
\]

Equivalently, if \(\delta:=\|P-Q\|_{\mathrm{TV}}\), then
\[
D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\|Q)\ge 2\delta^2.
\]

The key local scalar step is the Bernoulli inequality
\[
p\log\!\left(\frac{p}{q}\right)+(1-p)\log\!\left(\frac{1-p}{1-q}\right)\ge 2(p-q)^2,
\qquad 0<p,q<1,
\]
which appears after collapsing the general measures to a two-point partition.

nodes.title: Pinsker's inequality
nodes.informal_statement: Let $P,Q$ be probability measures on $(X,\mathcal{F})$ with $P\ll Q$. Then $D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\|Q)\ge 2\,\|P-Q\|_{\mathrm{TV}}^2$.
nodes.informal_proof_text: Set $\delta:=\|P-Q\|_{\mathrm{TV}}$. By the Hahn decomposition of $P-Q$, choose $A\in\mathcal{F}$ with $P(A)-Q(A)=\delta\ge 0$. Define the measurable map $T:=\mathbf{1}_A:X\to\{0,1\}$, and let $p:=P(A)$, $q:=Q(A)$, so $T_\#P=(p,1-p)$ and $T_\#Q=(q,1-q)$. By the data-processing inequality (DPI), $D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\|Q)\ge D_{\mathrm{KL}}(T_\#P\|T_\#Q)=p\log(p/q)+(1-p)\log((1-p)/(1-q))$. By the Bernoulli scalar inequality applied with $p-q=\delta$, the right-hand side is $\ge 2(p-q)^2=2\delta^2$. Hence $D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\|Q)\ge 2\delta^2=2\|P-Q\|_{\mathrm{TV}}^2$.

nodes.title: Data-processing inequality for KL divergence
nodes.informal_statement: Let $P,Q$ be probability measures on $(X,\mathcal{F})$ with $P\ll Q$, and let $T:(X,\mathcal{F})\to(Y,\mathcal{G})$ be any measurable map. Then $D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\|Q)\ge D_{\mathrm{KL}}(T_\#P\|T_\#Q)$.
nodes.informal_proof_text: This is the standard information-monotonicity property of relative entropy: applying a measurable map coarsens the $\sigma$-algebra and cannot increase relative entropy. It follows from Jensen's inequality (log-sum inequality) or from the chain rule for KL and non-negativity of conditional KL. In the binary pushforward case $T=\mathbf{1}_A$, this specializes to $D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\|Q)\ge p\log(p/q)+(1-p)\log((1-p)/(1-q))$ with $p=P(A)$, $q=Q(A)$.

nodes.title: Bernoulli/binary KL scalar inequality
nodes.informal_proof_text: Define $F_q(p):=p\log(p/q)+(1-p)\log((1-p)/(1-q))-2(p-q)^2$ for $p\in(0,1)$ with $q\in(0,1)$ fixed. By direct substitution $F_q(q)=0$. By direct computation $F_q'(q)=0$. By the convexity lemma for $F_q$, $F_q$ is convex on $(0,1)$, so $p=q$ is a global minimizer. Therefore $F_q(p)\ge F_q(q)=0$ for all $p\in(0,1)$.
nodes.formalization_rationale: Promoted after verified child 'bernoulli_ineq_refined_local_claim' certified a local core; attempting broader parent coverage.

theorem bernoulli_ineq_aristotle (p q : ℝ) (hp : p ∈ Ioo (0 : ℝ) 1) (hq : q ∈ Ioo (0 : ℝ) 1) :
    p * Real.log (p / q) + (1 - p) * Real.log ((1 - p) / (1 - q)) ≥ 2 * (p - q) ^ 2

nodes.title: Convexity of $F_q$ via second-derivative bound
nodes.informal_statement: For fixed $q\in(0,1)$, define $F_q(p):=p\log(p/q)+(1-p)\log((1-p)/(1-q))-2(p-q)^2$ on $p\in(0,1)$. Then $F_q''(p)=\tfrac{1}{p(1-p)}-4\ge 0$, so $F_q$ is convex on $(0,1)$.
nodes.informal_proof_text: Differentiating term by term: $\frac{d}{dp}[p\log(p/q)]=\log(p/q)+1$ and $\frac{d}{dp}[(1-p)\log((1-p)/(1-q))]=-\log((1-p)/(1-q))-1$, giving $F_q'(p)=\log(p(1-q)/(q(1-p)))-4(p-q)$. Differentiating again: $F_q''(p)=\tfrac{1}{p}+\tfrac{1}{1-p}-4=\tfrac{1}{p(1-p)}-4$. Since $p(1-p)\le\tfrac{1}{4}$ for all $p\in(0,1)$ (AM-GM), we have $\tfrac{1}{p(1-p)}\ge 4$, so $F_q''(p)\ge 0$ throughout $(0,1)$.
nodes.formalization_rationale: The second-derivative bound F_q''(p) = 1/(p(1-p)) - 4 >= 0 is a fully explicit, term-by-term calculus computation and a natural target for nlinarith or norm_num in Lean. Formalizing this node essentially proves bernoulli_ineq.

theorem fq_convex_aristotle (q : ℝ) (hq0 : 0 < q) (hq1 : q < 1) :
    ConvexOn ℝ (Ioo 0 1) (Fq q)

nodes.title: Convex function on (0,1) with a zero critical point is nonnegative
nodes.informal_statement: Let $G:(0,1)\to\mathbb{R}$ be convex and differentiable. If there exists $q\in(0,1)$ with $G(q)=0$ and $G'(q)=0$, then $G(p)\ge 0$ for all $p\in(0,1)$.
nodes.informal_proof_text: Convexity of $G$ implies that for every $p\in(0,1)$, $G(p)\ge G(q)+G'(q)(p-q)=0+0\cdot(p-q)=0$, where the first-order convexity condition $G(p)\ge G(q)+G'(q)(p-q)$ follows from convexity of $G$.
nodes.formalization_rationale: This is the concluding inferential step that transforms the convexity lemma (parent node) and the critical-point calculation (first proposal) into the desired inequality. It is strictly narrower than the full Bernoulli inequality claim because it factors out the analytic content into a clean, reusable lemma about convex functions, making the formal proof obligation precise and minimal.

formal_artifact.lean_statement:
theorem bernoulli_ineq_refined_local_claim_aristotle
    (G : ℝ → ℝ)
    (hconv : ConvexOn ℝ (Ioo 0 1) G)
    (hdiff : DifferentiableOn ℝ G (Ioo 0 1))
    (q : ℝ) (hq : q ∈ Ioo (0 : ℝ) 1)
    (hGq : G q = 0)
    (hG'q : deriv G q = 0)
    (p : ℝ) (hp : p ∈ Ioo (0 : ℝ) 1) :
    0 ≤ G p
formal_artifact.lean_code:
import Mathlib

/-!
# Convex function on (0,1) with a zero critical point is nonnegative

If G : ℝ → ℝ is convex on (0,1) and differentiable on (0,1), and there exists q ∈ (0,1)
with G(q) = 0 and G'(q) = 0, then G(p) ≥ 0 for all p ∈ (0,1).

The proof uses the first-order convexity condition:
  G(p) ≥ G(q) + G'(q)(p - q) = 0 + 0·(p - q) = 0.
-/

open Set in
theorem bernoulli_ineq_refined_local_claim_aristotle
    (G : ℝ → ℝ)
    (hconv : ConvexOn ℝ (Ioo 0 1) G)
    (hdiff : DifferentiableOn ℝ G (Ioo 0 1))
    (q : ℝ) (hq : q ∈ Ioo (0 : ℝ) 1)
    (hGq : G q = 0)
    (hG'q : deriv G q = 0)
    (p : ℝ) (hp : p ∈ Ioo (0 : ℝ) 1) :
    0 ≤ G p := by
  by_cases hp_lt_q : p < q;
  · have := hconv.slope_le_deriv ( show p ∈ Set.Ioo 0 1 from hp ) ( show q ∈ Set.Ioo 0 1 from hq ) hp_lt_q;
    simp_all +decide [ slope_def_field ];
    exact le_of_not_gt fun h => absurd ( this ( hdiff.differentiableAt ( Ioo_mem_nhds hq.1 hq.2 ) ) ) ( by rw [ div_le_iff₀ ] <;> linarith );
  · by_cases hp_gt_q : p > q;
    · have := hconv.deriv_le_slope ( hq ) ( hp ) hp_gt_q ( hdiff.differentiableAt ( Ioo_mem_nhds hq.1 hq.2 ) );
      simp_all +decide [ slope_def_field ];
      rw [ le_div_iff₀ ] at this <;> linarith;
    · grind
formal_artifact.faithfulness_notes: The Lean theorem encodes exactly the target node's claim: given G convex on (0,1), differentiable on (0,1), with a point q∈(0,1) where G(q)=0 and deriv G q=0, the conclusion is 0 ≤ G p for any p∈(0,1). The hypotheses and conclusion match the informal statement one-to-one, including the open-interval domain, the critical-point conditions, and the nonnegativity conclusion. No major aspect of the target is missing or weakened.

edges.label: reduces to binary KL
edges.explanation: DPI applied to T=1_A gives D_KL(P||Q) >= D_KL(T_#P||T_#Q), collapsing the problem to the two-point Bernoulli case.

edges.label: scalar lower bound closes the chain
edges.explanation: Bernoulli inequality bounds D_KL(T_#P||T_#Q) >= 2(p-q)^2 = 2*delta^2, completing the proof.

edges.label: refined_from
edges.explanation: This refined local claim was carved out from the broader sibling node as provenance, not as a proof dependency.

edges.label: uses
edges.explanation: This refined local claim depends on the broader supporting node it was carved out from.
